title: Class 2 A and EVA Pilot Candidates
display_title: Class 2 A and EVA Pilot Candidates
wikitext:
[]: Class 2-A and "EVA Pilot Candidates"             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Sep 19, 2012 8:41 pm It was mentioned the members of Class 2-A (Shinji's class with Rei, Asuka, Kensuke, Toji, Hikari, etc.) were all "candidates to become EVA Pilots". Well... There appears to be a problem here. The EVAs are supposed to have souls inserted into them (with the possible exception of the Mass Production EVA Units), and it is almost always that of the pilot's mother. This certainly stands as the case for Shinji, Asuka, and deducibly, Toji, as well as the fact the children in Class 2-A are all motherless, but there's at least 12-15 more people! It's unclear at which point an EVA Unit has its pilot designated, but in NGE, all EVA Units produced after Unit-05 are the MP Series. However, it is worth noting that NERV probably had some degree of knowledge/influence with regards to the MP Series, as Gendo is informed by an unnamed agent that their country will begin the construction EVA Unit-08. That particular EVA was known as a member of the MP Series in EoE. As well, the MP Series appear to use "Kaworu" dummy plugs as their controllers, so their exact capabilities are unknown. Then which units (if any) would the remaining children have theoretically been pilots for? This is probably superficial, but was it ever suggested that the pilot candidates would be used in combat against the Angels, or what would the purpose have been? Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Sep 19, 2012 11:09 pm The key word i think is "potential" pilots, in the sense that they could be apt to pilot an Evangelions instead of having an Evangelion with their names written on them. SEELE and NERV being the no-nonsense organizations they are would likely have way more back-ups than necessary in case anything happened, and then more. As for the MP series confusion (The unnamed agent telling Gendo their country will begin construction of Unit 8) I think it's important to mention there are a lot of varying circles/levels of knowledge of the conspiracy among the players/characters, i wouldn't be surprised if Gendo had a completely different idea of Unit 8 compared to what it actually was, or if it was the chinese goverment the one getting fooled. And aside from the myriad of secrecy and misinformation regarding the preparations for the Instrumentality project, there is always the "live concert" part, by which i mean that Anno himself said he didn't know from the start what story he wanted to make, so certain parts of the plot look iffy or fragmented because of that. And i think something like "The MP series is piloted by Shinji's classmates" is something that appeared in the wiki's theory and analysis page, but is intended to be scrapped in the finished film if memory doesn't fail me. You should look it up in the wiki.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Thu Sep 20, 2012 9:38 am Or maybe there's a dark, dusty room somewhere in the Geo-Front that has a bunch of cores inside it, one for every student in that class. Probably not just mother-souls, though. Seems like they would have figured that out. The time after Second Impact was chaotic. No one would notice if NERV or MARDUK or SEELE went around snatching people -- or possibly giving an out to people with terminal illness (if you're feeling generous).

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Sep 20, 2012 12:18 pm I think when Gendo is discussing unit 08 he is just trying to figure out how much time he has to get his own plans done. The closer they get to mass production means less time for him. As for the kids, they were all motherless and that makes them candidates; remember Kensuke tells Shinji that he doesn't have one either. I don't think NERV was planning to use every single kid in that class; they just needed a reserve, say if Asuka or Shinji were killed in combat they could grab the next one.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Sep 20, 2012 10:05 pm Dream wrote: TI think it's important to mention there are a lot of varying circles/levels of knowledge of the conspiracy among the players/characters

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Sep 30, 2012 12:13 am JoeD80 wrote: As for the kids, they were all motherless and that makes them candidates

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 30, 2012 12:21 am Sharaz Destler wrote: SEELE will stop at nothing to make sure its version of events plays out. Sleep on it, everyone. Sleep on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 30, 2012 3:38 am arael wrote: You don't mean that a set of assassins was sent by SEELE to kill random mothers and make it look like an accident but in a way they could obtain the soul so that they could flask it and then put it in a giant robot and until then keep the kids on watch so that they could keep then in the "Eva pilot fish tank" that was the class to have them picked at the proper time and with no apparent preparation that went back years and sweet days back?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 30, 2012 12:29 pm Sachi wrote: Nerv has within their inventory a core corresponding to each child with each respective mother's soul ready for activation

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 30, 2012 1:52 pm arael wrote: And how comes they came up with that soul?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Sep 30, 2012 2:12 pm Sachi wrote: I have no bloody clue. Fanwank something. I'll do the same:

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Sep 30, 2012 2:33 pm arael wrote: And how comes they came up with that soul?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 30, 2012 4:21 pm The aftermath of Second Impact would provide scope for bereavements to have happened quite naturally -- it depends exactly how long after the event that souls can be harvested (whether it's the Buddhist folklore 49 days or somewhat longer) whether some of the bereavements were unfortunate deliberates.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Sep 30, 2012 9:50 pm They lined up the kid's mothers and pushed them into a wood chipper with a crude, handmade sign reading "Gehirn Soul Collector" and no one will convince me otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Oct 01, 2012 3:35 pm I feel like the simultaneous or serial deaths of 20 or so of the mothers in Tokyo-III wouldn't have gone unnoticed by the general public. I think the kids in Class 2-A were just gathered because they are of the proper age to become Eva pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Oct 01, 2012 7:02 pm Let's look at some of the facts: The CI on Rei says that her synch rate is relatively low because she "does not have a mother". Also, she acts like she can pilot EVA 02 at any time. Shinji could also operate EVa 02, even if he needed Asuka's help to get usable results. Rei's synch rate with EVA 01 was the same as with EVA 00, but when Shinj gets into EVA 00, his synch rate is notably lower. The conclusion I'd draw is: ===> A suitable enough candidate should be able to operate any Eva unless the Soul inside objects to them or has other plans, but the own mother simply works BEST. This may also explain why Shinji ultimately trumped Asuka, Yui is completely in there, while EVA 02 contains a ripped-out fragment. Even if *something* got transfered over after Kyoko's eventual death, it probably isn't the same as her fully entering the EVA while still alive. Alternatively, EVA 02 may be more well-behaved because as a production model, it was mainly built to fight angels and as doomday device. Remember: Gendo *planned* for EVA 01 to grab the S2 engine. He may have loosened the security in some way. ===> Thus the kids in Shinji's class are chosen by certain criteria, like having been in-utero during SI, not having a living mother, and other part sciency, part psychological/metaphysical stuff. Maybe people who had their maternal bonding mumbo jumbo disrupted or prevented are receptive to forming that sort of "bond" with the Eva. They used the kids whose mothers were actually stuck in the EVAs because these are naturally more compatible - insert technobable about A10 nerve and AT-Fields. And I guess Rei qualifies by being part Angel part human and thus familiar to EVAs in a completely different way, together with her relation t5o whatever's in EVA 00. Also, Gendo needed an excuse for having her around/creating her. "She's for controlling the Lillith-Based Eva or the pesky prototype" sounds a whole lot better than "She's meant to hijack your TI plan"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 01, 2012 9:49 pm Chuckman wrote: They lined up the kid's mothers and pushed them into a wood chipper with a crude, handmade sign reading "Gehirn Soul Collector" and no one will convince me otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 02, 2012 3:18 am It'd probably just be easier to just grab kids who are always motherless. Seriously, there's no shortage. Especially after the biggest catastrophe in human history.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Oct 02, 2012 2:59 pm arael wrote: You don't mean that a set of assassins was sent by SEELE to kill random mothers and make it look like an accident but in a way they could obtain the soul so that they could flask it and then put it in a giant robot and until then keep the kids on watch so that they could keep then in the "Eva pilot fish tank" that was the class to have them picked at the proper time and with no apparent preparation that went back years and sweet days back?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Oct 02, 2012 4:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: It'd probably just be easier to just grab kids who are always motherless. Seriously, there's no shortage. Especially after the biggest catastrophe in human history.
